nodes.context: One percenter motorcycle club formed in the 1960s in San Bernardino, California. One of the largest and most powerful one-percenter biker gangs in the US.
Outlaw motorcycle club; Vagos members in Ontario patched over to them in 2015 before AOA disbanded in 2018.
Outlaw motorcycle club; involved in a shooting with Vagos at John Ascuaga's Nugget in Sparks, Nevada in 2011.
Motorcycle club involved in a shootout with Vagos on I-44 near Waynesville, Missouri in 2011.

edges.evidence_quote: Vagos in Ontario, Canada were reported to have patched over to Outlaws Motorcycle Club (American Outlaws Association) in 2015 before AOA disbanded in 2018.
edges.context: Vagos members in Ontario joined Outlaws Motorcycle Club by patching over in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: On September 23, 2011, Vagos members were involved in a shooting at John Ascuaga's Nugget in Sparks, Nevada, where Jeffrey Pettigrew, the president of the San Jose, California chapter of Hells Angels was killed, and Vagos members were wounded.
edges.context: Vagos and Hells Angels were involved in a violent shooting resulting in deaths and injuries.

edges.evidence_quote: On August 13, 2011, law enforcement authorities reported that the Vagos Motorcycle Club and the Galloping Goose Motorcycle Club were involved in a shootout which shut down traffic on I-44 near Waynesville, Missouri.
edges.context: Vagos and Galloping Goose were involved in a shootout on a highway.
